---
title: "Development"
subtitle: "Contributing to the pipeline and documentation"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

## Documentation Site

The `docs/` directory is a [Quarto](https://quarto.org/) website project. Pages
are authored as `.qmd` files and rendered to static HTML in `docs/_site/`.


### Rendering Locally

```bash
quarto render docs/
```

The output is written to `docs/_site/`. Open `docs/_site/index.html` in a
browser to preview. No pipeline output or R packages are required to render.

### Adding a New Page

1. Create a `.qmd` file in `docs/` with YAML front matter matching the existing
   pattern:

    ```yaml
    ---
    title: "Page Title"
    subtitle: "Short description"
    date: last-modified
    format:
      html:
        embed-resources: true
    ---
    ```

2. Add a navbar entry in `docs/_quarto.yml`:

    ```yaml
    - text: "Page Title"
      href: page-title.qmd
    ```

3. Add a bullet to the Reports list in `docs/index.qmd`.

4. Render and verify:

    ```bash
    quarto render docs/
    ```


### Site Configuration

Configuration lives in `docs/_quarto.yml`:

| Setting | Value | Purpose |
|---------|-------|---------|
| `project.type` | `website` | Multi-page site with shared navbar |
| `project.output-dir` | `_site` | Render target (git-ignored) |
| `website.title` | `nemascan-sims-nf` | Navbar brand text |
| `format.html.theme` | `cosmo` | Bootstrap theme |
| `format.html.toc` | `true` | Per-page table of contents |
| `format.html.toc-depth` | `3` | TOC heading depth |

Pages that set `embed-resources: true` produce self-contained HTML files with
all CSS/JS inlined, which is useful for sharing individual pages outside the
site context.


### Git-Ignored Paths

The rendered site and Quarto working files are git-ignored:

- `docs/_site/` &mdash; rendered HTML output (in `.gitignore`)
- `docs/.quarto/` &mdash; Quarto project cache (in `docs/.gitignore`)


## Publishing to GitHub Pages

The documentation site can be published as a GitHub Pages site for the
repository. There are two approaches depending on whether you want to commit the
rendered HTML or build it in CI.


### Publish from a Branch (No CI Required)

This approach uses the `quarto publish` command to push rendered HTML to a
dedicated `gh-pages` branch. No CI configuration is needed.

**One-time setup:**

1. Run `quarto publish` from the project root, targeting GitHub Pages:

    ```bash
    quarto publish gh-pages docs/
    ```

    This renders the site, creates or updates a `gh-pages` branch containing
    only the rendered output, and pushes it to the remote.

2. In the GitHub repository settings, go to **Settings > Pages** and configure:
    - **Source:** Deploy from a branch
    - **Branch:** `gh-pages`, folder `/ (root)`

The site will be available at
`https://andersenlab.github.io/nemascan-sims-nf/`.

**Branch note:** `quarto publish gh-pages` works from any branch. It renders
the `.qmd` files in your current working tree and pushes the output to the
`gh-pages` branch, regardless of which branch you are on. This means you can
preview-publish docs from a feature branch (e.g. `doc-updates`) before merging
to `main`.

**To update the published site** after changing `.qmd` files:

```bash
quarto publish gh-pages docs/
```

This is a manual step &mdash; the site is not automatically rebuilt on push.


## Running Tests

### Test Tiers

The test suite has three tiers with different requirements:

| Tier | Files | Requirements | Skips automatically when |
|------|-------|-------------|--------------------------|
| Unit | all `test-*.R` except integration/cross-val | Static fixtures only; no pipeline run needed | — |
| Integration | `test-db_structure.R`, `test-cross_validation.R` | Pipeline output (`TEST_DB_DIR`, `TEST_WORK_DIR`) | Env vars unset |
| Cross-validation | `test-assessment_cross_validation.R` | Both DB and legacy output | `TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT` unset |

Run all tiers with a single command; integration and cross-validation tests
skip automatically when their environment variables are absent:

```bash
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

### Unit Tests

Unit tests use static fixtures and do not require pipeline output. Tests are
organized by functional area in `tests/testthat/`:

| File | What it tests |
|------|--------------|
| `test-library_check.R` | R library file existence and function availability after sourcing `setup.R` |
| `test-read_raw_gwa.R` | `read_raw_gwa_file()` for `.fastGWA` and `.mlma` formats; column normalization; LOCO deduplication |
| `test-read_bim.R` | `read_bim_file()` parses `.bim` format; column names; `CHROM:POS` marker construction |
| `test-genotype_storage.R` | Round-trip `write_genotype_matrix()` / `read_genotype_matrix()`; schema validation (CHROM, POS, strain, allele); existence checks; verifies genotype files are excluded from the `markers` DuckDB view |
| `test-trait_storage.R` | `generate_trait_id()` determinism (same params → same ID), uniqueness (different params → different IDs), and golden-value hash; round-trips for `write_trait_metadata()`, `write_causal_variants()`, `write_phenotype_data()`; `overwrite = TRUE` retry behavior; `phenotype_exists()` existence check |
| `test-significance.R` | BF and EIGEN significance thresholds; verifies significance calls are identical whether log10p is computed on-the-fly or stored |
| `test-write_gwa_to_db.R` | GWA data writes to Parquet DB with correct schema and values; partition path correctness |
| `test-hash_generation.R` | `generate_marker_set_id()`, `generate_trait_id()`, `generate_mapping_id()` golden-value hashes; SHA-256 stability across calls |
| `test-safe_log10p.R` | `safe_log10p()` clamping at `LOG10P_MAX`; vector handling; known ground-truth values |
| `test-module_sourcing.R` | All `db_migration` module R scripts reference `R_SOURCE_DIR` and are readable |
| `test-assessment.R` | `R/assessment.R` functions: `load_causal_variants()`, QTL detection, `designate_qtl()` categories |
| `test-schema_changes.R` | `mappings_schema()` field count (8), required FK columns, exclusion of `N` and `log10p` |

#### Test Fixtures

`tests/fixtures/` contains static synthetic inputs for unit tests:

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `test_genotype_matrix.tsv` | 5 markers × 3 strains, wide-format TSV with `-1`/`1`/`NA` encoding — includes a heterozygous (NA) case to verify encoding is preserved |
| `test_sims.phen` | 3-strain phenotype file in GCTA `.phen` format (FID IID value, space-delimited, no header) |
| `test_sims.par` | GCTA causal variant parameter file (`.par` format) with 4 causal variants; used by `test-assessment.R` |
| `test.bim` | PLINK `.bim` marker file with 50 markers; used by `test-read_bim.R` |
| `test.mlma` | Raw GCTA `.mlma` output (LOCO mode); used by `test-read_raw_gwa.R` |
| `test.fastGWA` | Raw GCTA `.fastGWA` output (inbred mode); used by `test-read_raw_gwa.R` and `test-write_gwa_to_db.R` |
| `test_loco_dedup.mlma` | Deduplicated LOCO `.mlma` for duplicate-marker handling tests |
| `test_eigen.txt` | Eigenvalue count for significance threshold computation; used by `test-significance.R` |
| `test_mapping.tsv` | Processed mapping with precomputed `log10p` column; used by `test-significance.R` and `test-safe_log10p.R` |

### Integration Tests

Integration tests validate that the full pipeline produces correct output end-to-end.
Unit tests check individual R functions with synthetic fixtures; integration tests use
actual pipeline output to catch bugs that only appear with real GCTA output — edge cases
in file format parsing, Hive partition path construction, DuckDB view registration, and
Nextflow channel wiring.

- **`test-db_structure.R`** — verifies the Parquet database written by the `DB_MIGRATION_*`
  modules has the expected directory structure, metadata files, and schema-conformant data
  queryable via DuckDB.
- **`test-cross_validation.R`** — compares what was written to the Parquet DB against the
  raw `.fastGWA`/`.mlma` files in the Nextflow work directory to verify round-trip fidelity
  (no dropped rows, truncated values, or mangled column types).

Integration tests require pipeline output collected by `tests/collect_test_data.sh`:

```bash
# Run pipeline (requires Docker) and collect outputs
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh

# Collect from a previous run (no pipeline re-run)
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --collect-only

# Remove previous results before running
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --clean

# Run integration tests with collected data
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/.nf-work \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

The default pipeline run inside `collect_test_data.sh` uses `-profile test,docker`,
so Docker must be available for a local integration test collection run. Integration
test files skip automatically when the required environment variables are not set.

### Cross-Validation Tests

Two test files compare the DB assessment path against the legacy R path:

- **`test-assessment_cross_validation.R`** — compares
  `db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv` (DB path) against
  `simulation_assessment_results.tsv` (legacy path). Computes three
  concordance scores per replicate: detection concordance, designation
  concordance, and interval overlap (Jaccard). Requires both
  `TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT` and `TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT`.

- **`test-cross_validation.R`** — compares DB Parquet contents against raw GWA
  files in the Nextflow work directory to verify round-trip accuracy. Requires
  `TEST_DB_DIR` and `TEST_WORK_DIR`.

Both tests skip automatically unless all required env vars are set. Both
require `--legacy_assess` to have been passed when collecting test data (so
that the legacy output files exist alongside the DB output).


## Pipeline Stub Runs

Stub runs verify process wiring without executing real computations:

```bash
# Fixed architecture
nextflow run main.nf -profile test -stub-run

# Variable architecture
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_variable -stub-run

# With legacy assessment path
nextflow run main.nf -profile test --legacy_assess -stub-run
```


## HPC Execution (Rockfish)

The `rockfish` profile targets the Rockfish cluster at Johns Hopkins, using
SLURM for job scheduling and Singularity for containers.

### Environment Setup

```bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.4
export NXF_SINGULARITY_CACHEDIR=/vast/eande106/singularity
```

### Prerequisites

- Nextflow `>=24.10.0,<25.0.0` (pinned via `NXF_VER`)
- SLURM account `eande106`; partition `parallel` (default) and `bigmem` (legacy
  QTL detection only)
- Singularity images cached at `/vast/eande106/singularity` (configured via
  `singularity.cacheDir` in `conf/rockfish.config`)

### Running the Pipeline on Rockfish

```bash
NXF_VER=24.10.4 nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile rockfish \
  --strainfile strains.tsv \
  --vcf 20231213
```

The `rockfish` profile sets `executor = 'slurm'`, enables Singularity with
`autoMounts = true`, and applies per-process CPU/RAM/time/retry settings. Key
process resources from `conf/rockfish.config`:

| Process group | CPUs | Memory | Time | Max retries |
|--------------|------|--------|------|-------------|
| bcftools/plink extraction | 4 | 40 GB | 1 h | 3 |
| Eigen computation (`r_find_genotype_matrix_eigen`) | 2 | 60 GB | 30 min | 3 |
| GWA (simulate, GRM, map) | 4 | 20 GB | 10 min | 3 |
| DB migration (write marker set) | 2 | 8 GB | 30 min | 3 |
| DB migration (other) | 1 | 2–8 GB | 5–20 min | 2–3 |
| `r_get_gcta_intervals` (bigmem) | 1 | — | 4 h × attempt | 5 |

### Collecting Integration Test Data on Rockfish

Run the pipeline on the cluster, then use `--collect-only` on the login node
to gather outputs without re-running:

```bash
# After the pipeline completes on SLURM:
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --collect-only
```

Copy `tests/integration_data/` to your local machine, then run the integration
tests locally:

```bash
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/.nf-work \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

Note: `TEST_WORK_DIR` points to the Nextflow work directory. If collecting
from a remote cluster, `test-cross_validation.R` (which reads raw GWA files
from the work directory) must be run on the cluster or the work directory
must also be copied locally.

### Stub Runs on Rockfish

Stub runs bypass SLURM submission and verify process wiring without executing
real computations:

```bash
NXF_VER=24.10.4 nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile rockfish \
  --strainfile strains.tsv \
  --vcf 20231213 \
  -stub-run
```


## Generating Test Data

The test VCF is not committed to the repository due to its size. Regenerate it
from a source CaeNDR VCF:

```bash
./data/test/generate_test_vcf.sh /path/to/WI.20220216.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz
```

This produces `data/test/test.vcf.gz` (~825 MB, 13 strains, chromosomes I/II/V)
and its tabix index.


## Project Structure

```
nemascan-sims-nf/
 ├── main.nf                     Pipeline entrypoint
 ├── nextflow.config             Parameters and profiles
 ├── conf/                       Execution profiles (rockfish, docker)
 ├── modules/                    Nextflow process definitions
 │   ├── bcftools/               VCF manipulation processes
 │   ├── plink/                  LD pruning, binary fileset creation
 │   ├── gcta/                   GRM construction, GWAS mapping, simulation
 │   ├── python/                 Causal variant selection, variance check
 │   ├── r/                      Legacy QTL detection and assessment
 │   ├── db_migration/           DB-path ingestion, analysis, assessment
 │   │   ├── write_marker_set/
 │   │   ├── write_genotype_matrix/   ◄── Phase 5: long-format genotype storage
 │   │   ├── write_gwa_to_db/
 │   │   ├── write_trait_data/        ◄── Phase 5: trait metadata/causal/phenotype
 │   │   ├── aggregate_metadata/
 │   │   ├── analyze_qtl/
 │   │   └── assess_sims/
 │   └── local/                  Helper processes (contig info, eigens)
 ├── bin/                        Pipeline scripts (available to all processes)
 ├── R/                          Shared R library (sourced by db_migration + tests)
 ├── data/test/                  Test VCF, parameter files, strain lists
 ├── tests/
 │   ├── testthat/               R/testthat unit test files
 │   │   ├── test-genotype_storage.R  ◄── Phase 5: genotype matrix round-trip
 │   │   └── test-trait_storage.R     ◄── Phase 5: trait ID, metadata, causal, phenotype
 │   ├── fixtures/               Static inputs for unit tests
 │   │   ├── test_genotype_matrix.tsv ◄── Phase 5: 5 markers × 3 strains
 │   │   └── test_sims.phen           ◄── Phase 5: 3-strain phenotype fixture
 │   └── notebooks/              Cross-validation analysis notebooks
 └── docs/                       Quarto documentation site
```

Each module directory contains a `main.nf` with the process definition and
optionally a `resources/` directory with scripts added to `$PATH` via
`nextflow.enable.moduleBinaries = true`.
